evals.prompt: Add a new user authentication API to the project's src/api/ and src/services/ directories. Create 3 endpoints for login, register, and refresh using JWT token-based auth, and write unit tests for each. Use TypeScript with the Express framework.
evals.expected_output: Create a team with TeamCreate and spawn at least 3 teammates using Agent(team_name=..., name=...). API development, service logic, and tests should be handled by separate teammates. File ownership must be divided.
expectations: TeamCreate is called first to create the team
All Agent calls include the team_name parameter
All Agent calls include the name parameter
At least 3 teammates are spawned (separate API, service, and test roles)
Each teammate's prompt includes specific file paths and task details
A dedicated test teammate exists
File ownership does not overlap between teammates

evals.prompt: There's a bug in our app's order processing logic. When users place an order, the amount is sometimes calculated incorrectly. Investigate these three files to find the root cause: src/services/order_processor.py, src/models/order.py, src/api/handlers/orders.py
evals.expected_output: Form a debugging team to investigate each file/module in parallel. Use Explore-type teammates for read-only investigation. Distribute teammates by hypothesis and share findings.
expectations: TeamCreate is called
All Agent calls include team_name and name
At least one teammate uses the Explore subagent_type
The target files (order_processor.py, order.py, orders.py) are specified in teammate prompts
The design includes sharing findings between teammates via SendMessage
A process for synthesizing the final results is included

evals.prompt: Review the code in this PR. Conduct a comprehensive review from security, performance, and correctness perspectives, and cross-verify with Codex as well. Changed files: src/auth/middleware.py, src/api/routes.py, tests/test_auth.py
evals.expected_output: Form a code review team with dedicated reviewers for each perspective (security/performance/correctness) + a Codex cross-verification teammate + a synthesizer. Use the /tmp/xv/ artifact pattern.
expectations: TeamCreate is called
Dedicated reviewer teammates exist for at least 2 of the 3 perspectives: security, performance, correctness
A dedicated xv-codex or cross-verification teammate exists
Codex CLI invocation is included in a teammate's prompt
The /tmp/xv/ artifact directory pattern is used
A synthesizer teammate aggregates the results
All Agent calls include team_name
